page.title: Pools
page.description: Every liquidity pool discovered by the indexer across active Meteora protocols. The list is ordered by discovery time, most recent first.

empty.title: No pools observed yet
empty.description: The indexer hasn't seen any pool activity on the configured allowlist. Pools will appear here as soon as on-chain events are picked up.

error.title: Unable to load pools
error.timeout: The upstream service took too long to respond. Try again in a moment.
error.unavailable: The upstream service is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a moment.
error.bad_request: The request was rejected by the upstream service. The cursor may have expired - start over from the first page.
error.unexpected: An unexpected error occurred while loading the pools.

stateEmpty.title: No state observed yet
stateEmpty.description: This pool exists in the indexer but no swap or liquidity event has been captured for it. The current-state projection is built from those two event types - fee or reward claims alone don't populate it.

feedEmpty.noSwaps: No swap events recorded for this pool yet.
feedEmpty.noLiquidity: No liquidity events recorded for this pool yet.

feedError.timeout: The upstream service took too long to respond.
feedError.unavailable: The upstream service is temporarily unavailable.
feedError.bad_request: The request was rejected by the upstream service. The cursor may have expired.

meta.title: About -  Yog-Scope
meta.description: Yog-Scope is an open source real-time observation platform for Meteora liquidity pools on Solana.

hero.lead: Yog-Scope is an open source platform that observes liquidity activity on Meteora pools as it happens on Solana, reconstructs what occurred, and exposes the result through an API and a dashboard.

problem.title: The problem
problem.body: Meteora's pools are an active piece of Solana DeFi: swaps, liquidity events, fee claims, and rewards happen continuously across hundreds of pools. The on-chain state changes block by block, but reconstructing what actually happened - across time, across pools, with enriched context - is not something the chain offers out of the box. There is no public, real-time, queryable history of Meteora's activity.

approach.title: The approach
approach.body: Yog-Scope sits on a persistent WebSocket connection to a Solana RPC and observes Meteora's events as they are emitted. Each event is decoded from its on-chain format, enriched with token metadata and USD prices, and persisted in a time-series database. The platform is focused on Meteora as a protocol - its decoding is anchored on Meteora's event format rather than a generic multi-DEX adapter - and the historical record accumulates as the platform runs.

audience.title: Who it's for
audience.body: Three audiences. Liquidity providers who want to see, in real time, what is happening on the pools where they hold positions. Analysts and traders who want a queryable history of pool activity to detect patterns, large movements, or imbalances. Developers and builders who want a typed HTTP API exposing Meteora's activity, without having to build their own indexer.

availability.title: How it's available
availability.body: The platform is released under the MIT license. The code is public and the dashboard is free to use; this is intentional and not going to change. The current scope is Meteora's DAMM v2 pools - coverage of the other Meteora products (DLMM, DAMM v1, Stake2Earn) is planned but not yet delivered. Premium features may be added later, but the open infrastructure and the free public access stay.

behind.title: Who is behind it
behind.body: Yog-Scope is built and maintained by <awsd>AWSD</awsd>, an independent software company based in France. The source code lives on <github>GitHub</github>.

meta.title: Privacy —  Yog-Scope
meta.description: How  Yog-Scope handles your data. Short answer: almost nothing is collected.

header.title: What we do with your data.
header.lead: Yog-Scope is a read-only public dashboard. It does not require an account, does not use analytics, and does not track visitors. This page explains in plain terms the very limited data the platform handles.

inShort.title: In short
inShort.body: Yog-Scope does not require an account, does not use analytics, and does not set non-essential cookies. The only personal data the platform ever sees are technical server logs (kept briefly for security and operations) and any email you choose to send to the contact address. That is the whole list.

responsible.title: Who is responsible
responsible.body: The data controller is [AWSD legal name], a company registered in France ([SIREN]), whose registered office is at [registered address]. For any privacy-related question, you can reach out at <email>[contact-email]</email>.

collected.title: What we collect, and why
collected.body: The web server hosting  Yog-Scope records standard technical logs — IP address, user agent, requested URL, response status, timestamp — strictly to keep the service running and to detect abuse. These logs are retained for a maximum of twelve months and are not shared with anyone outside the hosting provider. If you send an email to the contact address, that email and its contents are kept for the time strictly needed to handle your request. No other personal data is collected. There is no account system, no tracking pixel, no third-party analytics, and no advertising.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: The site does not set any cookie that requires consent. A small number of strictly necessary technical cookies may be used to remember your language preference and to make the site work correctly — these are exempt from consent under the ePrivacy directive. The list will be updated if non-essential cookies are ever introduced.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: Under the GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, port, or object to the processing of your personal data, and to define directives about your data after your death. To exercise any of these rights, write to <email>[contact-email]</email>. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the French data protection authority (CNIL, www.cnil.fr) if you believe your rights are not respected.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.body: This policy may evolve, for instance if the platform starts using analytics or introduces an account system. When it changes, the "last updated" date at the top of the page is revised; substantive changes will be highlighted on the site.
